--- title: "The Preventable Chargeback: A Prevention Roadmap" subtitle: "Chargeback Prediction Model — Cinderhaven Data Platform" format: html: toc: true toc-depth: 2 pdf: toc: false ---
Prevention Roadmap
## $446\,200 in annual chargebacks. 73% is preventable. Cinderhaven carries **$446\,200** in annual retailer chargebacks. Grouping them by root cause and applying a fixed preventability fraction to each category identifies **$324\,888** — 73% of the total — as addressable through four fixable upstream data conditions. This figure is an assumption-driven estimate (historical loss per archetype × its preventability fraction), not a model prediction. The scoring workflow names the specific data condition driving each purchase order's risk. This report presents the root-cause breakdown, the ranked prevention roadmap, and the economics of fixing versus disputing. *This analysis uses Cinderhaven synthetic demonstration data to illustrate a repeatable methodology. The numbers, proportions, and root-cause distribution reflect a realistic CPG brand profile.* --- ## Root-cause breakdown ::: {#fig-by-archetype .cell execution_count=2} ::: {#fig-by-archetype-1 .cell-output .cell-output-display} ``` Unable to display output for mime type(s): text/html ``` Historical chargeback amount by root cause. Logistics overage — primarily missing case dimensions and case weight — accounts for the single largest preventable category. ::: ::: {#fig-by-archetype-2 .cell-output .cell-output-display} ``` Unable to display output for mime type(s): text/html ``` ::: ::: --- ## Margin math The table below translates each root cause into a dollar figure: what it cost historically, what fraction is preventable with targeted data fixes, and the estimated annual recovery. ::: {#tbl-margin-math .cell execution_count=3} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ```{=html}
Root cause Historical loss Preventable Prevention value Chargeback count
Logistics Overage $245K 70% $171K 2284
Data Compliance Error $143K 80% $114K 300
ASN Timing Infraction $42K 70% $29K 273
Pricing Discrepancy $17K 60% $10K 16
``` ::: ::: --- ## Ranked prevention roadmap ::: {.cell execution_count=4} ::: {#fig-prevention-value .cell-output .cell-output-display} ``` Unable to display output for mime type(s): text/html ``` Prevention value by root cause — estimated annual dollar recovery from targeted data fixes. Logistics overage tops the list at over $240K, driven primarily by case dimension and case weight gaps in the product master. ::: ::: The actions required to capture this prevention value are data management tasks, not operational overhauls. The roadmap below names the specific fix for each root cause. ::: {#tbl-roadmap .cell execution_count=5} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=5} ```{=html}
Rank Root cause Annual prevention value Required action
1 Logistics Overage $171K Audit pick-and-pack procedures; add case-count verification before palletizing
2 Data Compliance Error $114K Populate missing barcodes (GTIN-14, UPC) and verify label compliance before shipment
3 ASN Timing Infraction $29K Submit Advance Ship Notices within the retailer-required lead time window
4 Pricing Discrepancy $10K Reconcile PO unit prices against approved price lists before invoicing
``` ::: ::: --- ## Recovery versus prevention The deductions team currently disputes chargebacks after they post. The industry win rate on retailer disputes is 30–45%. At a 40% win rate, recovering $446\,200 in annual chargebacks yields roughly **$178\,480** — before dispute labor costs. Prevention requires no dispute process. Fixing the data conditions identified above eliminates the chargeback at source. A companion model demonstrates pre-ship scoring, with one caveat stated plainly. On Cinderhaven's *real* chargebacks the features carry no predictive signal (AUC ≈ 0.50): real chargebacks are effectively random with respect to data quality. The model is therefore trained on **synthetic** labels drawn from a known risk formula (risk ∝ ASN lateness × data-quality gaps), and it recovers that formula. On held-out synthetic data its AUC is **0\.70** — a *ranking* measure: it ranks a charged-back shipment above a clean one 70% of the time. At a 0.5 threshold it flags 72% of chargebacks (recall). AUC is not the flag rate, and predictive skill on the real target is effectively zero — the durable value is the prevention roadmap, not the score. The math is straightforward: **$324\,888** in preventable chargebacks recovered at 100% is worth more than $446\,200 recovered at 40%, and it eliminates the working capital drag of post-ship disputes.
This analysis was produced using Cinderhaven synthetic demonstration data. All dollar figures, chargeback counts, and root-cause distributions are illustrative of a real methodology, not actual Cinderhaven financials. Real chargebacks carry no predictive signal (AUC ≈ 0.50); the model is trained on synthetic labels and the reported AUC is measured on held-out synthetic data.
